---
title: "4.11 Set and Get Values in 2D Arrays"
subtitle: "2D Array Creation and Access"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Set Values in a 2D Array

### `array[row][col]`

The square brackets `[row][col]` are used to access and modify an element in a 2D array.

When accessing the element at `arr[first][second]`, the first index is used for rows, the second index is used for columns.

To explicitly put a value in an array, use assignment statements with the name of the array followed by the row index and column index.

## Set Value Example

### Assignment into a cell

```java
int[][] ticketInfo = new int[2][3];
ticketInfo[0][0] = 15;
```

`ticketInfo[0][0]` means row index `0`, column index `0`.

## Coding Exercise

### `activecode:: twoDArraySet`

Try the code below.

When you print a two dimensional array you just get the reference to the object.

Edit the code to add in an extra row to the `seatingChart` and add your name and a friend's name in the columns of this extra row using assignment statements.

## TwoDArraySet Source Code

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
public class TwoDArraySet
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        // declare arrays
        int[][] ticketInfo;
        String[][] seatingChart;

        // create arrays
        ticketInfo = new int[2][3];
        seatingChart = new String[3][2];

        // initialize the array elements
        ticketInfo[0][0] = 15;
        ticketInfo[0][1] = 10;
        ticketInfo[0][2] = 15;
        ticketInfo[1][0] = 25;
        ticketInfo[1][1] = 20;
        ticketInfo[1][2] = 25;
        seatingChart[0][0] = "Jamal";
        seatingChart[0][1] = "Maria";
        seatingChart[1][0] = "Jacob";
        seatingChart[1][1] = "Suzy";
        seatingChart[2][0] = "Emma";
        seatingChart[2][1] = "Luke";

        // print the contents
        System.out.println(ticketInfo);
        System.out.println(seatingChart);
    }
}
```
:::

## TwoDArraySet Test Targets

### Runestone checks

The code should print two 2D array references.

The code should contain:

```java
new String[4][2]
```

The code should also contain assignments to:

```java
seatingChart[3][0]
seatingChart[3][1]
```

## Quick Check

### `mchoice:: qa2dab_1`

Which of the following sets the value for the 3rd row and 2nd column of a 2D array called `nums`?

A. `nums[3][2] = 5;`  
B. `nums[1][2] = 5;`  
C. `nums[2][1] = 5;`  
D. `nums[2][3] = 5;`

## Quick Check: Answer

### qa2dab_1 reasoning

Correct answer: **C. `nums[2][1] = 5;`**

This sets the value of the 3rd row and 2nd column because indices start at `0` and the row index comes first.

## Get a Value from a 2D Array

### Read from a cell

To get the value in a 2D array, give the name of the array followed by the row and column indices in square brackets.

```java
int[][] ticketInfo = { {25, 20, 25}, {25, 20, 25} };
String[][] seatingInfo = { {"Jamal", "Maria"}, {"Jake", "Suzy"}, {"Emma", "Luke"} };
int value = ticketInfo[1][0];
String name = seatingInfo[0][1];
```

## Quick Check

### `mchoice:: qa2dab_2`

What is the value of `name` after the code executes?

Options:

A. `Jamal`  
B. `Maria`  
C. `Jake`  
D. `Suzy`  
E. `Emma`

## Quick Check: Answer

### qa2dab_2 reasoning

Correct answer: **B. `Maria`**

`Maria` is the value of `seatingInfo[0][1]`.

## Coding Exercise

### `activecode:: twoDArrayInitGet`

Add another row to `seatingInfo` initialized to your name and a friend's name.

Get these names out of the array using the correct indices and then print them out.

## TwoDArrayInitGet Source Code

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
public class TwoDArrayInitGet
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        String[][] seatingInfo =
        {
            {"Jamal", "Maria"},
            {"Jake", "Suzy"},
            {"Emma", "Luke"}
        };
        String name = seatingInfo[0][0];
        System.out.println(name + " is at [0,0]");
    }
}
```
:::

## TwoDArrayInitGet Test Targets

### Original output and added row checks

The original output should contain:

```text
Jamal is at [0,0]
```

After adding a new row, one printed line should contain:

```text
[3,0]
```

Another printed line should contain:

```text
[3,1]
```

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

- use `[row][col]` with the row index first
- use assignment statements to set 2D array values
- explain why printing a 2D array directly prints a reference-like value
- retrieve a value with the same `[row][col]` pattern
- choose `[2][1]` for the 3rd row and 2nd column

## End

### 4.11 Set and Get Values in 2D Arrays

Next: row/column length and ASCII Art challenge.
